degraded: classify_complexity flagged cross_component from the DESCRIPTION prose (pipeline-phase-validator + campaign-runner keywords), which set the medium floor + the integration_coverage enforcement. The F11 check_integration_coverage RECOMPUTES the flag from the diff (merge-base..HEAD), and this diff touches NONE of CROSS_COMPONENT_FILE_PATTERNS (iterate_checks.py / decision_log_gate.py / sub-iterate-runner.md are not in that set), so the gate does not require the integration behavior. A real-scenario integration test (test_finalization_integrity_integration.py, category:integration) was written regardless, because the change genuinely composes the F3 -> F5c -> F11 producers/consumer and is worth proving end-to-end.

bloat: iterate_checks.py grew past its baseline current (1109) while adding the new gate + the drop-content tightening. Resolved by EXTRACTING the ADR/decision-log integrity cluster (check_adr_in_iterate_history + _drop_carries_adr + check_iterate_no_direct_decision_log) into a new <300-line module verifiers/decision_log_gate.py and re-exporting; iterate_checks.py ratcheted DOWN to 1062 (baseline updated to match). The runner contract doc landed at exactly its grandfathered 479 by condensing F3/F5c to reference references/F3.md + references/F5c.md and tightening the F2 browser-verify prose (all commands preserved) — no bloat-exception ADR needed.

surface_verification.justification: Framework-internal change: the F11 iterate-verifier logic (verifiers/iterate_checks.py + the extracted verifiers/decision_log_gate.py), the sub-iterate-runner contract doc + its result-JSON schema, and the finalization/reference docs. No web/cli/api application surface exists or changed — the monorepo is library-scoped SDLC tooling, and the affected CLIs are internal pipeline steps, not a user-facing product. Verified empirically by the full shared test suite (4712 passed, 11 skipped) + the iterate plugin suite (497 passed) + a real-producer->verifier integration test (test_finalization_integrity_integration.py) that drives write_decision_drop -> append_iterate_entry -> verify_iterate_finalization end-to-end against a real git repo and asserts BOTH the forbidden direct-decision_log.md-write path AND the empty-drop path fail closed.

behaviors.behavior: (b) run-id ADR identity is accepted only when a decision-drop actually CARRIES the ADR (parses as an object + run_id matches + non-empty decision)
(b) an empty/placeholder {} drop (the shape of a silently-lost ADR) is REJECTED, not passed
(b) a drop that parses and matches run_id but has an empty/whitespace decision is still rejected
(b) post-aggregation, a **Run-ID:** line for the run in decision_log.md satisfies run-id identity
(b) a run-id identity with NEITHER a carrying drop NOR a Run-ID line fails closed
(c) an iterate commit that modifies .shipwright/agent_docs/decision_log.md fails the new F11 gate (ERROR)
(c) an iterate commit that does not touch decision_log.md passes the gate
(c) the gate SKIPs (does not fail) when no --commit is supplied or git is unavailable
(c) the gate is scoped to the exact agent-doc path — a lookalike file merely named decision_log does not trip it
(c) the new gate is wired into run_all_checks (the F11 verifier check list) and skips cleanly on the happy path

(a) the sub-iterate-runner contract routes F3 through write_decision_drop.py (NOT write_decision_log.py), carries F5c (append_iterate_entry.py) as a mandatory step, and names the no-direct-decision_log F11 gate

(a) the runner result.json schema admits the OPTIONAL finalization record (f3_decision_drop / f5c_iterate_entry / verifier), and a sample carrying it validates while legacy results without it stay valid

INTEGRATION: the real producers compose — write_decision_drop (F3) -> append_iterate_entry (F5c) -> verify_iterate_finalization (F11) go GREEN on a proper run, RED when the iterate commit writes decision_log.md directly, and RED when the drop is an empty placeholder

test_completeness.note: AC4 (retention docs made consumer-explicit + the webui follow-up triage anchor trg-92c0c36b) and AC5 (docs/hooks-and-pipeline.md validator registry) are documentation/data outcomes with no executable behavior, so they are not enumerated as testable behaviors. The enumeration covers the three code ACs (AC1 runner contract + schema, AC2 verifier tightening, AC3 new gate); enumeration_basis is intentionally omitted because the AC set mixes code and doc-only criteria. The (b) numbered-ADR backward-compat branch was deliberately left intact (see the spec's rejected alternative) and is still covered by the pre-existing test_adr_check_passes_when_both_config_and_log_align + the dual-mode tests, which stayed green.
